LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS LOUIS VUITTON NORTH AMERICA, INC., AND, TERANY JACKSON MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DENIED AS TO FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BATTERY AND THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR ASSAULT. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED WHETHER JACKSON HAD THE REQUISITE INTENT TO INJURE PLAINTIFF AND WHETHER PLAINTIFF WAS IN REASONABLE APPREHENSION OF JACKSON BEFORE THE CONTACT. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DENIED AS TO THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT WHETHER JACKSON'S CONDUCT EXTREME AND OUTRAGEOUS. PLAINTIFF CLAIMS JACKSON HARASSED AND PHYSICALLY STRUCK HER. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT WHETHER LOUIS VUITTON'S CONDUCT AFTER THE INCIDENT EXTREME AND OUTRAGEOUS AND CONTRIBUTED TO PLAINTIFF'S EMOTIONAL INJURY. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION GRANTED AS TO FOURTH AND EIGHTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR WRONGFUL TERMINATION AND VIOLATION OF THE LABOR CODE, SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR RETALIATION, AND NINTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DISABILITY DISCRIMINATION. NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL ADVISED LOUIS VUITTON THAT PLAINTIFF WAS PHYSICALLY INCAPABLE OF RETURNING TO WORK AND PLAINTIFF DID NOT RESPOND TO DEFENDANT'S INJURY INTO WHAT REASONABLE ACCOMMODATION IT COULD PROVIDE. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION GRANTED AS TO SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF IMPLIED COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT. NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT RAISED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLEAD AN ENFORCEABLE CONTRACT AND CANNOT REBUT THE PRESUMPTION OF AT-WILL EMPLOYMENT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Separate Trials Of Issues; Memorandum Of P/A In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Oct-20-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS LOUIS VUITTON NORTH AMERICA, INC., and, TERANY JACKSON Motion for Separate Trials of Issues. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for separate trials of issues (bifurcation) granted in part and denied in part. Granted as to amount of punitive damages. Denied as to remainder of issues without prejudice to re-raising the motion before the trial judge. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-20-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS LOUIS VUITTON NORTH AMERICA, INC., and, TERANY JACKSON MOTION FOR SUMMARY
JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary judgment denied. Motion for summary adjudication denied as to first cause of action for battery and the second cause of action for assault. Triable issue of fact raised whether Jackson had the requisite intent to injure plaintiff and whether plaintiff was in reasonable apprehension of Jackson before the contact. Summary adjudication denied as to third cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress. Triable issue of fact whether Jackson's conduct extreme and outrageous. Plaintiff claims Jackson harassed and physically struck her. Triable issue of fact whether Louis Vuitton's conduct after the incident extreme and outrageous and contributed to plaintiff's emotional injury. Summary adjudication granted as to fourth and eighth causes of action for wrongful termination and violation of the Labor Code, sixth cause of action for retaliation, and ninth cause of action for disability discrimination. No triable issue of fact raised. Plaintiff's counsel advised Louis Vuitton that plaintiff was physically incapable of returning to work and plaintiff did not respond to defendant's injury into what reasonable accommodation it could provide. Summary adjudication granted as to seventh cause of action for breach of implied covenant of good faith and fifth cause of action for breach of contract. No triable issue of material fact raised. Plaintiff has not plead an enforceable contract and cannot rebut the presumption of at-will employment. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600
